retrieved_result: How Cost of Living Raises Work Average Cost-of-Living Raise Prev NEXTThe level of inflation has varied widely and so have cost of living raises. Regular Social Security cost-of-living adjustments (COLAs) began in 1975 when inflation was running high. The first COLA was 8 percent. The raise reached a high of 14.3 percent in 1980.
